data.title: Coinbase CEO: Will personally engage in research on Bitcoin's quantum resistance problem
data.description: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong stated that he will personally engage in research on quantum-resistant Bitcoin, emphasizing the industry's need to expedite solutions. Coinbase Chief Security Officer Philip Martin previously pointed out that as quantum computing research progresses, the industry needs to accelerate its response to potential threats. He stated that post-quantum cryptography is feasible but still in its early stages, and hasty implementation could introduce new security risks. Furthermore, consensus needs to be reached on issues such as how to handle wallets that haven't completed their migration. In addition, Coinbase has already undertaken related infrastructure upgrades and research, and is encouraging developers and the community to collaborate on quantum-resistant solutions.

data.title: Bitcoin Faces Potential Downside Risk as BTC Risk Indicator Rises
data.description: Bitcoin Vector has indicated that Bitcoin is currently at a critical juncture, where the potential for downside risk may increase. According to NS3.AI, the platform noted that its BTC Risk indicator has not transitioned into a low-risk phase and is instead on the rise again.

data.title: Swiss CPI Growth Slows in March, Falling Short of Expectations
data.description: Switzerland's Consumer Price Index (CPI) for March increased by 0.2%, according to Jin10, which is below the anticipated 0.5% and the previous month's rate of 0.60%. The slower growth in CPI suggests a potential easing of inflationary pressures in the Swiss economy. Analysts had expected a higher rate, reflecting ongoing concerns about inflation trends in the region. The latest data may influence monetary policy decisions by the Swiss National Bank as it assesses the economic outlook.

data.title: Iran Vows Continued Resistance Amid Ongoing Conflict
data.description: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Baghaei stated that Iran will persist in its resistance as long as the conflict and assaults on Iranian citizens and cities persist. According to NS3.AI, Baghaei emphasized that the nation stands united against what he described as an unjust and aggressive war.

data.title: Web3 Carnival Guide for April Released
data.description: Foresight News posted on X (formerly Twitter) that as the second quarter begins, the highly anticipated Web3 Carnival Summit is approaching. Attendees are encouraged to save the guide to surrounding activities, which will be continuously updated in the coming weeks to help participants navigate the event efficiently and effortlessly.

data.title: Dollar Index Rises Amid U.S. Military Movements in Middle East
data.description: On April 2, Jin10 reported that the dollar index increased during the Asian afternoon trading session as market expectations grew regarding potential U.S. military actions against Iran. According to Jin10, analysts from the Commonwealth Bank of Australia's global economics and market research division noted in a report that the U.S. is reportedly doubling its fleet of A-10 attack aircraft in the Middle East. They added that the movement of military assets and troops to the region raises the possibility of a ground invasion. Given the dollar's status as a safe-haven currency, they remain optimistic about its performance.

data.title: Russia Imposes Gasoline Export Ban Until July
data.description: Russia has announced a ban on gasoline exports, effective until the end of July. According to BlockBeats, the decision was reported by the International Telegraph Agency on April 2. The measure aims to stabilize domestic fuel supplies amid fluctuating market conditions. The ban is expected to impact gasoline producers and could lead to adjustments in global fuel markets.

data.title: HyperCore Executes Significant HYPE Token Buyback and Burn
data.description:
On April 2, Hyperliquid's official data revealed that on April 1, HyperCore repurchased 43,749.54 HYPE tokens at an average price of approximately $36.77. According to BlockBeats, the same day saw the distribution of 26,657 HYPE tokens to stakers and 24 validator nodes. After accounting for these distributions, a net total of 17,092 HYPE tokens were permanently burned, resulting in a daily net deflation of about 17,000 tokens. If this pace continues, the annual deflation rate could reach approximately 6.15 million tokens.

In comparison, Solana experiences an annual inflation of approximately 25.19 million SOL due to staking and validator node rewards. HyperCore's buyback efficiency is inversely related to price, helping to mitigate supply pressure during market cycles.

data.title: Alabama Grants Legal Status to DAOs with New Legislation
data.description:
Alabama has become the second U.S. state to grant legal status to Decentralized Autonomous Organizations (DAOs), following Wyoming. According to BlockBeats, the state's governor signed the Decentralized Non-Registered Nonprofit Association Act on April 2, providing DAOs with legal recognition and limited liability protection. This legislation allows DAOs to function as full legal entities, enabling them to own property, engage in legal proceedings, and enter contracts, while protecting individual members and managers from personal liability. To qualify, a DAO must have at least 100 members and be formed for a common nonprofit purpose, such as governing blockchain networks or smart contract systems.

The head of policy at a16z Crypto emphasized that decentralized governance is central to the future of cryptocurrency, and this law offers decentralized communities the certainty needed to build, govern, and expand in the real world. A similar bill in West Virginia has passed the House and is awaiting the governor's signature.

data.title: Poland Central Bank Member: No Immediate Rate Cuts Expected
data.description: Poland's central bank member, Livynuk, has stated that there should be no expectation of interest rate cuts in the near future. According to Jin10, Livynuk emphasized that if inflation trends show a downward trajectory, the bank will consider raising rates instead. This statement reflects the bank's cautious approach to monetary policy amid ongoing economic uncertainties.

data.title: Axe Compute Reports FY2025 Financial Results, Highlights Decentralized GPU Network Progress
data.description:
On April 2, Axe Compute, a Nasdaq-listed company (NASDAQ: AGPU), released its FY2025 financial report, marking a significant milestone as the first publicly traded company to disclose decentralized computing power business developments to institutional investors. According to BlockBeats, the report reveals that the company executed contracts totaling approximately $12 million over the past 30 days, with estimated monthly revenue for Q2 at $835,000 and a client base exceeding 20 enterprise customers.

Following the release of the financial report, AGPU experienced a single-day peak increase of 152%, with trading volume surpassing the 20-day average by 900 times, reaching a total daily trading volume of over $300 million.

All enterprise computing power deployments are delivered through the Aethir network, which spans 94 countries and over 200 nodes, utilizing more than 435,000 GPU containers and delivering a cumulative total of over 1.8 billion computing hours.

data.title: Chinese Stock Indices Experience Significant Decline
data.description: Chinese stock indices saw a notable decline, with the Shenzhen Component Index dropping over 2%. According to ChainCatcher, the ChiNext Index fell by 2.8%, and the Shanghai Composite Index decreased by 1.12%. Across the market, more than 4,600 stocks experienced a downturn.

data.title: Vitalik shared his local private LLM solution, emphasizing privacy and security as priorities.
data.description: Vitalik Buterin shared his localized, private LLM deployment solution up to April 2026. The core objective is to prioritize privacy, security, and autonomy, minimizing opportunities for remote models and external services to access personal data. This is achieved through local inference, local file storage, and sandbox isolation to reduce the risks of data breaches, model jailbreaking, and malicious content exploitation. On the hardware side, he tested solutions including a laptop with an NVIDIA 5090 GPU, an AMD Ryzen AI Max Pro with 128 GB unified memory, and DGX Spark, using Qwen3.5 35B and 122B models for local inference. The 5090 laptop achieved approximately 90 tokens/s with the 35B model, the AMD solution approximately 51 tokens/s, and the DGX Spark approximately 60 tokens/s. Vitalik stated that he prefers to build local AI environments based on high-performance laptops, while using tools such as llama-server, llama-swap, and NixOS to build the overall workflow.

data.title: KOSPI Experiences Significant Drop, Triggering Sell-Side Sidecar Activation
data.description: The Korea Exchange activated a sell-side sidecar following a notable decline in the KOSPI index, which fell 5.17% to 5,195.61. According to NS3.AI, this marks the first activation of a sell-side sidecar in eight days, with the previous occurrence on March 23. The mechanism is designed to stabilize the market by temporarily halting trading, allowing investors to reassess their positions amid heightened volatility.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Proposes Localized AI Deployment Strategy for Enhanced Privacy and Security
data.description:
Vitalik Buterin has outlined a strategy for deploying localized and private large language models (LLMs) by April 2026. According to Odaily, the plan emphasizes privacy, security, and autonomy, aiming to minimize the exposure of personal data to remote models and external services. The approach includes local inference, local file storage, and sandbox isolation to reduce risks of data leaks, model jailbreaks, and malicious content exploitation.

In terms of hardware, Buterin tested various configurations, including a laptop with an NVIDIA 5090 GPU, an AMD Ryzen AI Max Pro device with 128 GB unified memory, and DGX Spark setups. He utilized Qwen3.5 35B and 122B models for local inference, achieving approximately 90 tokens per second with the 5090 laptop, around 51 tokens per second with the AMD setup, and about 60 tokens per second with DGX Spark. Buterin expressed a preference for building local AI environments based on high-performance laptops, using tools like llama-server, llama-swap, and NixOS to establish the overall workflow.

data.title: Renaiss Protocol Platform Surpasses $7 Million in Total Trading Volume
data.description:
On April 2, the BNB Chain ecosystem's collectibles infrastructure project, Renaiss Protocol, reported a total trading volume exceeding $7 million. According to BlockBeats, the platform has seen continuous growth since its Beta testing began in November 2025.

Recent data indicates that in March, the trading volume on the Renaiss platform accelerated significantly, driven by mechanisms such as Infinite Gacha (TCG Infinite Card Machine), with approximately $3 million added in a single month. This milestone comes just 10 days after surpassing $6 million.

Alongside the growing trading volume, Renaiss recently completed its latest round of community rewards distribution, awarding over $40,000 to more than 400 users for their contributions to community growth and ecosystem promotion. This initiative aims to foster a positive cycle between community engagement and platform activity. Since the Beta testing phase, the platform has distributed over $163,100 in community rewards and user incentives, including USDT, physical cards, card boxes, and various forms of giveaways, airdrops, and buybacks.

Additionally, Renaiss has unveiled its Beta 2.0 roadmap and continues to advance its liquidity mechanism, SuperLiquid, along with the Web3's first TCG-Fi mechanism and AI Agent capabilities. As trading volume expands, community incentives are implemented, and product development progresses steadily, Renaiss is enhancing the liquidity foundation and user participation experience in the on-chain collectibles market.

data.title: Bitcoin Analyst Predicts Bear Market Will Drop Below $60,000
data.description:
On April 2, renowned technical analyst Killa (@KillaXBT) released a long-term logarithmic chart of Bitcoin. According to BlockBeats, the chart highlights the 'perfect halving lower bound' and the upper and lower band ratios, asserting that Bitcoin's current bear market will inevitably fall below $60,000.

Killa, known for his focus on BTC quantitative trading, previously predicted the peak of the current bull market in May 2025. He has amassed over 180,000 followers on the X platform.

data.title: KOSPI Index Experiences Volatility with Consecutive Circuit Breakers
data.description:
On April 2, the KOSPI index in South Korea activated its circuit breaker mechanism following a 5% drop in KOSPI 200 futures, according to BlockBeats. This led to a temporary halt in algorithmic trading for five minutes.

The previous day, the KOSPI index had surged over 5% at the opening, triggering the circuit breaker, and at one point during the session, it rose to 8.72%.

data.title: Iranian military spokesman: Larger, wider, and more destructive attacks are coming.
data.description: According to Iran's Tasnim News Agency, an Iranian military spokesperson stated that a larger, wider, and more destructive attack is imminent. The war will continue until "the enemy surrenders and permanently regrets it." (Jin Shi)

data.title: Gold fell below $4,600, down 3.26% on the day.
data.description: According to Gate.com data, XAUUSD (gold) has fallen below 4600 USD and is currently trading at 4599.97 USD, with the daily decline widening to 3.26%.
